body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/` **Triggered by:** commit 21456f8 (#57862) by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed Commit 21456f8 added conditional preview API version filtering in `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`. Previously, `ResourceVersions` may have included preview versions even when the default was GA. Now: - **If the default API version is GA** ŌåÆ only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews are excluded) - **If the default API version is itself a preview** ŌåÆ all versions (GA + preview) are listed The generated test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this new filtering, and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` was updated to point to the commit that introduced this behavior. ## Gaps Found - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` ŌĆö The "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section says *"Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order"* without explaining that preview versions **are** included when the default version is itself a preview. - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` ŌĆö Section 6 "Output Types" describes `ResourceVersions` only as *"nested class with GA API version constants"*, omitting the conditional logic that governs which versions are included. - Neither document explains what constitutes a "preview" version (i.e., the `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic ŌĆö version strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, etc.).
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` Edit the "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section. Locate the paragraph: ```markdown Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order: ``` Replace it with: ```markdown Lists supported API versions in reverse chronological order. The set of included versions depends on the default (latest) API version for the resource: - **When the default version is GA** ŌĆö only GA versions are included; preview versions are filtered out. - **When the default version is itself a preview** ŌĆö all versions (GA and preview) are included. A version is considered a preview if its string contains `preview`, `alpha`, or `beta` (case-insensitive). ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` In Section 6 "Output Types" ŌåÆ "Resource Provider" bullet list, locate: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants ``` Replace with: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with API version constants. When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes required ŌĆö these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain. ### Step 3: Verify README structure These are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. No standard section structure check applies. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands from the repo root. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` ŌĆö confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check). No snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions
